prose:
Fukui reactivity analysis of pyridine performed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier standard, PySCF backend) in gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Three electronic states were computed (neutral singlet, singly oxidized doublet, singly reduced doublet) to generate Fukui indices via Mulliken partial charge differences. The most electrophilic site is nitrogen (f⁺ = 0.484), indicating highest susceptibility to nucleophilic attack. The most nucleophilic sites are the meta carbons (f⁻ = 0.118 at atoms 3–4), indicating highest susceptibility to electrophilic attack. The nitrogen atom also shows the strongest dual descriptor (0.420), confirming it as the site of greatest overall reactivity. All SCF calculations converged cleanly, and charge conservation checks (sum of f⁺ and f⁻ both equal 1.0) passed, validating the result. No warnings reported. Geometry was user-supplied and not re-optimized at this DFT level.</prose>
